content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of length NNN.

You are allowed to perform the following operation on it **at most once**:


Choose an element AiA_iAi​ and change it to Ai+1A_i+1Ai​+1.
Find the **maximum** count of subarrays having **at least one even** element after performing the operation at most once.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer 𝑁𝑁N, denoting the size of the array 𝐴𝐴A.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers  A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2,\ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, denoting the elements of the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the **maximum** count of subarrays having **at least one even** element after performing the operation at most once.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111:

Without using the operation, the subarrays having at least one even element are [2],[2,1],[2,1,3],[2,1,3,4],[1,3,4],[3,4],[4][2], [2,1], [2,1,3], [2,1,3,4], [1,3,4], [3,4], [4][2],[2,1],[2,1,3],[2,1,3,4],[1,3,4],[3,4],[4].
After performing the operation on the A2A_2A2​, the array will become, A=[2,2,3,4]A = [2,2,3,4]A=[2,2,3,4], and the subarrays having at least one even element are [2],[2,2],[2,2,3],[2,2,3,4],[2],[2,3],[2,3,4],[3,4],[4][2], [2,2], [2,2,3], [2,2,3,4], [2], [2,3], [2,3,4], [3,4], [4][2],[2,2],[2,2,3],[2,2,3,4],[2],[2,3],[2,3,4],[3,4],[4].

Thus, our final answer will be 9.
It can be shown that this is the maximum possible count we can obtain.
